raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The national conversation surrounding mental illness and the heightened risks within the Military and Veteran populations has intensified in recent years.",
    "Research and funds have been increasingly directed towards identifying and treating mental illness among servicemembers.",
    "California servicemembers are subject to prosecution if they attempt or commit suicide under the Uniform Code of Military Justice (UCMJ).",
    "AB 2273 is a bill aimed at decriminalizing suicide attempts among servicemembers in California.",
    "AB 2273, if passed, would apply to approximately 20,980 servicemembers in California.",
    "77 percent of respondents in a survey by Iraq and Afghanistan Veterans of America sought care when suggested to do so.",
    "Concerns about a mental health diagnosis affecting their career have discouraged many servicemembers from seeking care.",
    "Under the UCMJ, servicemembers may be criminally prosecuted for attempting suicide, contributing to their reluctance to seek help.",
    "Active outreach and assistance for mental health disorders have been a priority of the California Military Department in recent years.",
    "In 2015, the California Military Department made over 1500 referrals for mental health supportive services."
  ]
}
```
